title: RFC: Default Payment Method (Backend — Credit Application, Post-Credit & Setting Page)

td.p: Mini PRD: Set GoPay Later as Default Payment Method across flows (local mirror: output/Default Payment Method/prd.md)

Backend only. Covers (1) credit-application flow opt-in capture, (2) post-credit application of the default, and (3) the setting-page read/write API. Transaction-flow nudge (PRD §B, US5–US9) is explicitly excluded.
Default-payment-method setting owned by paylater-user-service; BFF orchestrates read/write; lending-adapter triggers application on credit-approval callback.

ul.li: PRD (Lark)
Local PRD mirror: output/Default Payment Method/prd.md

td.p: GoPay Later (the pay-later product this RFC concerns).
A per-user, per-platform flag indicating GPL should be the pre-selected payment instrument on Gojek / GoPay checkout surfaces.
A GPL-whitelisted user who has not yet submitted / been approved for a credit application.
The side-effect chain that runs after a credit-application-success callback is received by lending-adapter.
Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.5 service owning per-user GPL data (DDL module gtfpaylater). DDD layout: shared/infrastructure/domain/application/web/scheduler.
Kotlin / Spring Boot 3.1 WebFlux BFF. Reactive Feign clients. Owns client-facing orchestration only.
Kotlin / Spring Boot 2.7 WebFlux adapter that receives the upstream lender (BizEnd/MAB) credit-application callback and runs the post-credit flow.
Existing enum value in gpl-bff UserSettingFeature and a disabled entry in toggle-configs.json — scaffolding this RFC activates.

p: GoPay Later is live in QRIS (CPM since Sep 2025; MPM ON-US Jan; OFF-US Feb). As of April there are 485k eligible users but only 11% adopted. The PRD wants to grow adoption by letting users set GPL as their default payment method across three flows: acquisition, transaction, and account-management (settings).

This RFC is scoped to the backend work for two of those three flows — the credit-application / post-credit flow and the setting page — and explicitly excludes the transaction nudge flow (PRD §B, US5–US9). The transaction nudge is tracked separately and will consume the read API defined here.

Key ownership decision (per engineering context): the GPL default-payment-method setting is owned by paylater-user-service. We will not call GoPay APIs to store or read this setting; GoPay-side checkout propagation is handled by a separate integration (see Open Questions).
Source: output/Default Payment Method/prd.md (full PRD). Only the in-scope backend-relevant user stories are summarised below.

td.p: Persist the user's opt-in decision at credit-application submit time (before approval). Toggle off by default.
Backend assigns Treatment 1 / 2 via gojek_customer_id % 2; configurable rollout 50% → 100%; global kill-switch.
On credit-application-success callback, apply the cached opt-in (from US1) as the live default. If the user toggles on the post-approval screen instead, persist that decision too.
US4 — Approved users who already set default
Read API returns default = true so clients render GPL pre-selected / labelled "Default".
Cache-on-confirm, risk-reject handling, PN fallback, backward compatibility.
No — excluded (separate RFC). Reuses the read/write API defined here.
US10 — Set default from GPL homepage / settings
Write API: user toggles GPL default on/off from the GPL settings screen.
US11 — Real-time fetch of default status
Read API: BFF returns the current default status real-time. Deviation: source of truth is paylater-user-service, not GoPay (see Open Questions OQ-1).
Setting GPL as default overwrites any prior default; next GoPay transaction uses GPL. Propagation to GoPay checkout widget is out of scope here.

p: Detailed, file-backed findings per repository are in the Repository Analysis section below. Summary:

ul.li: No default-payment-method storage exists anywhere. Neither paylater-user-service, gpl-bff, nor lending-adapter has a table, column, entity, DTO, or endpoint for a user-level default payment method.

gpl-bff already has scaffolding. UserSettingFeature.DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION and a toggle-configs.json entry exist but are show:false. The PATCH /account-management/settings/toggle path currently writes to the bizend (/internal/account-management/settings), not to paylater-user-service.
gpl-bff → paylater-user-service client exists but exposes only one method (SSOF eligibility). The base URL is configured (gpl-paylater-user-service.base-url).

lending-adapter receives the credit-application-success callback at POST /lending/internal/credit-application/callback and runs a post-credit flow that already calls gpl-bff /internal/credit-application/callback. It does not call paylater-user-service today.

paylater-user-service has no inbound credit-success listener; the only post-credit-success hook is the synchronous upsertUserInfo path driven by an upstream REST call. Schema changes are applied outside the repo (no Flyway/Liquibase files).

p: Findings are file-backed. Paths are relative to each repo root unless prefixed with ~/project/.
h2: paylater-user-service (Java 21, Spring Boot 3.5, MySQL + MyBatis-Plus, RabbitMQ, OpenFeign)
p: Repo: ~/project/paylater-user-service/ | Build: build.gradle:3-5,180-184 (Spring Boot 3.5.0, Spring Cloud 2025.0.0, JDK 21) | Layout: settings.gradle:3-8 declares shared, infrastructure, domain, application, web, scheduler (note: domain depends on infrastructure — inverted DDD, domain/build.gradle:6).

td.p: None. No table/column/entity/endpoint. Repo-wide search for defaultPayment|default_payment|PaymentMethod|paymentChannel returns zero source matches.
Repayment channel/method — recorded per-repayment-event, not per-user.
Flat columns on user_info (gopay_user_id, bill_day, pay_day, account_status). No user_setting/preference table exists.
Stores status, callback_status, type (CREATE/INCREASE), external_platform_id. Read-only domain service — no event publish, no listener.
Synchronous upsertUserInfo triggered by upstream REST POST /user-service/internal/user/user-info; on creditStatus == SUCCESS updates the existing user_info row.
GoToPin-only (PIN challenge/verify). No method to list payment methods or read/write a default — consistent with owning this setting locally.
Internal controllers under /user-service/internal/.... Partner-specific controllers gated by @ConditionalOnPartner (GTFPAYLATER|TTPL|TTPL_RISK_SHARE).
RabbitMQ only (no Kafka). 3 inbound consumers, all TTPL repayment/risk — none for credit-application-success.
No Flyway / Liquibase / .sql files. DDL implied by MyBatis-Plus @TableName/@TableField. Migrations applied out-of-repo.
Spring Cloud OpenFeign (15 clients). No gRPC.

h2: gpl-bff (Kotlin 1.7, Spring Boot 3.1 WebFlux, Reactive Feign, R2DBC/Postgres)
p: Repo: ~/project/gpl-bff/ | Build: build.gradle:4-7,17 (Spring Boot 3.1.2, Kotlin 1.7.22, JDK 17, WebFlux + Reactive Feign 4.0.0) | App: src/main/kotlin/com/gofin/gpl/bff/.

td.p: Enum value already declared; static config entry exists but show:false.
PATCH /toggle writes to bizend /internal/account-management/settings, not to paylater-user-service. mapBizendSettingsToUserFeature does not map DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION.
Exists, Reactive Feign, single method (SSOF). Base URL env-driven.
fetchToggles resolves gofinId via identity-info, fetches persisted settings, zips SSOF eligibility, merges static config — exactly the pattern to extend for the default-payment-method toggle.
POST /internal/credit-application/callback → push notification today. This is the endpoint lending-adapter already calls in its post-credit flow.

Inbound x-user-id header → identity-info POST /lending/v1/user/identity-info → IdentityInfoData(gofinId, gojekCustomerId, gojekGopayAccountId, ...). Outbound headers forwarded explicitly per Feign method.
RestApiResponse<T> sealed type; WebClientException(BffError) from per-client error decoders; global advice in GlobalRestExceptionHandler.

h2: lending-adapter (Kotlin 1.6, Spring Boot 2.7 WebFlux, WebClient, R2DBC/MySQL, Kafka)
p: Repo: ~/project/lending-adapter/ | Build: build.gradle.kts:4,6-7,19,30 (Spring Boot 2.7.12, Kotlin 1.6.21, JDK 17, WebFlux, reactor-kafka) | App: src/main/kotlin/com/gofin/cleo/lendingadapter/; web base-path /lending (application.yaml:143-144).

td.p: POST /lending/internal/credit-application/callback. Resolves GofinUser by accountId, dispatches to a CreditApplicationServiceV2 by applicationType.

Updates gofinUser state, then runs 3 side-effects in parallel via Mono.zip: Tokopedia callback, Kafka CREDIT_APPLICATION_RESULT publish, and GPL BFF callback POST /internal/credit-application/callback. No default-payment-method step exists today.
WebClient gplBffClient, submitCreditResult(...) with circuit breaker. Base URL configured.
None. No client, no configured URL. Closest GoPay-side downstream is gpl-bff.base-url.
None. No dedup table/lock; CallbackData.hasReceived is metadata-only, never read to short-circuit. Duplicate callback re-runs the whole flow.
Both scaffolded but empty. No existing callback API spec.

ul.li: paylater-user-service owns a new user_default_payment_setting table (MySQL, gtfpaylater datasource) keyed by gofin/gopay user id, storing is_default_enabled, opt_in_source (ONBOARDING_REVIEW / POST_APPROVAL / SETTINGS_PAGE), treatment_group, and audit timestamps.

Two new internal endpoints on paylater-user-service: a read (GET) and an idempotent write/apply (POST) for the default-payment-method setting, under /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method.

gpl-bff GET /account-management/settings/toggles additionally fetches the default-payment-method status from paylater-user-service and surfaces the (existing) DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION toggle with show:true behind a feature flag.
gpl-bff PATCH /account-management/settings/toggle for DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION is redirected from bizend to paylater-user-service.

gpl-bff exposes a new internal endpoint POST /internal/credit-application/default-payment-method/apply (called by lending-adapter on approval) that forwards the apply request to paylater-user-service.

lending-adapter post-credit flow gains a 4th parallel side-effect: call gpl-bff POST /internal/credit-application/default-payment-method/apply so the cached onboarding opt-in is applied on success. Idempotent.
Experiment modulo logic (US2) lives in paylater-user-service with a config-driven rollout percentage and a global kill-switch.
New user_default_payment_setting table + PO + mapper + domain model in paylater-user-service.
Read & write/apply internal APIs on paylater-user-service.
Experiment modulo + rollout config + kill-switch in paylater-user-service.
BFF toggle read enrichment + write redirection for DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION.
BFF internal apply endpoint consumed by lending-adapter.
lending-adapter post-credit side-effect to apply the cached default on approval.
Feature flag, observability, idempotency for the new apply path.
Transaction nudge flow (PRD US5–US9): cache-on-confirm, risk-reject handling, PN fallback, backward-compat gating. Separate RFC. Will reuse the read/write API defined here.

GoPay checkout widget propagation: how GPL actually becomes the pre-selected instrument on the GoPay payment widget (US4 "Default label" at checkout) is a GoPay-side integration, tracked under Open Question OQ-2.
Frontend / client work: acquisition review page UI, post-approval screen UI, settings screen UI.
Tokopedia default-payment-method (explicitly out of scope in the PRD).
Data migration of any pre-existing default setting (none exists).

p.b: Recommended approach: route every read/write of the default-payment-method setting through paylater-user-service, and trigger the post-credit apply via the existing lending-adapter → gpl-bff call chain.

p: Rationale: (1) respects the ownership boundary set by engineering — the GPL user setting lives in the GPL domain, not in GoPay or bizend; (2) reuses the existing gpl-bff → paylater-user-service Reactive Feign client and the existing lending-adapter → gpl-bff callback path, so no new cross-service network topology; (3) the BFF stays a thin orchestrator, matching the ToggleSettingService.fetchToggles aggregation pattern already in place.
What we will NOT do: add a lending-adapter → paylater-user-service direct client (considered, rejected — see Approach #2), call GoPay to store the setting, or backfill via bizend.

td.p: Source of truth for the default-payment-method setting; experiment modulo logic; read + apply APIs.
Client-facing /account-management/settings entry point (read enrichment + write redirection); internal apply endpoint for lending-adapter.
Credit-application-success callback; new post-credit side-effect that calls gpl-bff to apply the cached default.

whiteboard:
sequenceDiagram autonumber participant App as Gojek/GoPay App participant BFF as gpl-bff participant PUS as paylater-user-service participant LA as lending-adapter participant BizEnd as BizEnd / MAB
rect rgb(235, 248, 255) Note over App,BFF: US1/US2 — Acquisition submit (cache opt-in) App->>BFF: POST /v1/user/submit (x-user-id, optInDefault=true, treatmentGroup) BFF->>PUS: POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method (optInSource=ONBOARDING_REVIEW) PUS-->>BFF: 200 OK (cached, isApplied=false) BFF->>LA: submit credit application (existing) end
rect rgb(255, 246, 235) Note over BizEnd,PUS: US3 — Post-credit apply (on approval) BizEnd->>LA: POST /lending/internal/credit-application/callback (status=SUCCESS) LA->>LA: updateGofinUser state par LA->>BFF: POST /internal/credit-application/callback (existing) and LA->>BFF: POST /internal/credit-application/default-payment-method/apply BFF->>PUS: POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method/apply PUS->>PUS: is_default_enabled = cached optIn, isApplied=true (idempotent) PUS-->>BFF: 200 OK BFF-->>LA: 200 OK and LA->>LA: Kafka publish CREDIT_APPLICATION_RESULT (existing) end end
rect rgb(240, 255, 235) Note over App,PUS: US10/US11/US12 — Settings page read & write App->>BFF: GET /account-management/settings/toggles BFF->>PUS: GET /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method PUS-->>BFF: { isDefaultEnabled, optInSource } BFF-->>App: toggles incl. DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION (show=true behind FF) App->>BFF: PATCH /account-management/settings/toggle (DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION, enabled) BFF->>PUS: POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method (optInSource=SETTINGS_PAGE) PUS-->>BFF: 200 OK BFF-->>App: 200 OK end
h1: User Story: US1+US2 — Acquisition opt-in capture & experiment modulo
p: As a GPL virgin user on the acquisition review page, I want my "set GPL as default" toggle decision and my treatment group to be persisted at submit, so that it can be applied automatically once my credit application is approved.

td.p: Whitelisted virgin user, treatment resolved by gojek_customer_id % 2
User submits the credit application with opt-in on
BFF forwards opt-in + treatment to paylater-user-service; row created with is_default_enabled=true, is_applied=false, opt_in_source=ONBOARDING_REVIEW
Row created with is_default_enabled=false (or absent, defaulting false on read)
Feature disabled globally; no row written, BFF returns toggle show=false
All eligible users assigned a treatment group

p: BFF resolves the gofin id (existing GofinUserService) and calls a new POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method on paylater-user-service. paylater-user-service computes the treatment group from gojek_customer_id, enforces the rollout percentage + kill-switch from config, and upserts the user_default_payment_setting row.

td.p: Unique key — one row per user

p: paylater-user-service (new, internal):

ul.li: POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method — upsert opt-in + treatment. Body: { gofinUserId, isDefaultEnabled, optInSource }. Idempotent on gofin_user_id.
GET /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method?gofinUserId= — returns { isDefaultEnabled, isApplied, optInSource, treatmentGroup }.
POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method/apply — sets is_applied=true and finalises is_default_enabled from the cached opt-in. Idempotent; called from the post-credit flow.

p: None. Reuses the existing synchronous BFF→PUS Feign path. (An alternative Kafka fan-out was considered and rejected — paylater-user-service has no Kafka consumer; adding one would breach the ownership boundary.)

ul.li: Single owner of setting + experiment logic
Reuses existing Feign client & BFF aggregation pattern
No new broker dependency
Apply path is synchronous in the post-credit flow (mitigated by idempotency + circuit breaker)
DDL applied out-of-repo (no in-repo migration tooling)

p: Add a new lending-adapter → paylater-user-service WebClient and skip the BFF hop for the apply step.

ul.li: One fewer network hop on the apply path
Creates a new cross-service dependency not present today
Bypasses the BFF orchestration boundary the team has established
Diverges apply (LA→PUS) from read/write (BFF→PUS) — two integration paths to maintain

h1: User Story: US3+US4 — Post-approval apply & read-back
p: As a just-approved GPL user, I want my cached onboarding opt-in to be applied on approval (or to set it on the post-approval screen), so that GPL is my default payment method immediately.

td.p: User opted-in on the review page, credit just approved
is_applied=true, is_default_enabled=true; subsequent read returns default=true
User did not opt-in on review page
User toggles on the post-approval screen
BFF calls write API with optInSource=POST_APPROVAL; is_applied=true
No-op after first success; row unchanged, response still 200

p: lending-adapter PKYCCreditApplicationService.handleCreditApplicationCallback gains a 4th branch in the existing Mono.zip at PKYCCreditApplicationService.kt:792: call gplBffClient.applyDefaultPaymentMethod(...) → POST /internal/credit-application/default-payment-method/apply. gpl-bff adds that internal endpoint, which forwards to paylater-user-service POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method/apply.

Idempotency: the apply endpoint sets is_applied=true only if false (conditional UPDATE), so duplicate callbacks are safe. The post-approval-screen write (AC 3.2) reuses the same write API as US1 with optInSource=POST_APPROVAL and forces is_applied=true (the user is already approved).
h1: User Story: US10+US11+US12 — Settings page read, write & overwrite
p: As an approved GPL user, I want to toggle GPL as my default payment method from the GPL settings screen and see it reflected in real time, so that it is consistent across all GoPay surfaces.

td.p: User has never set a default
BFF calls PUS write API optInSource=SETTINGS_PAGE; is_default_enabled=true, is_applied=true
Default was set on another entry point
Toggle reflects the real-time value from PUS (no caching)
User had another payment method as default
GPL default overwrites; PUS is the source of truth for the GPL-side flag

p: Extend ToggleSettingService.fetchToggles (ToggleSettingService.kt:58-89) to additionally call a new gplPaylaterUserServiceClient.getDefaultPaymentMethod(gofinId) and merge it into the toggles map for DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION. Flip toggle-configs.json entry to show:true behind a feature flag evaluated at runtime.

Reroute ToggleSettingService.toggle (ToggleSettingService.kt:91-112): for feature == DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION, call gplPaylaterUserServiceClient.upsertDefaultPaymentMethod(...) instead of bizEndClient.saveUserSetting(...). Other features keep their bizend path unchanged.

ul.li: DDL migration for user_default_payment_setting on the gtfpaylater MySQL datasource — applied out-of-repo (no in-repo migration tooling). Coordinate with the DBA / release manager.
Feature flag (e.g. default-payment-method.enabled, default-payment-method.rollout-pct) on paylater-user-service and a matching BFF flag to gate show:true.
Frontend teams to consume the toggles API and the new apply path on the post-approval screen — not blocking this backend RFC but needed for end-to-end US3/US10.
GoPay checkout integration for US4 "Default label" propagation (Open Question OQ-2) — not blocking the setting/storage work here.

callout: Implementation tasks. Each carries implicit acceptance criteria from the AC tables above. Tasks are grouped by repo; order reflects the dependency chain (PUS first, then BFF, then LA).

td.p: Add user_default_payment_setting DDL on gtfpaylater (PK, unique gofin_user_id, is_default_enabled, is_applied, opt_in_source, treatment_group, timestamps). Coordinate out-of-repo migration.
Add PO UserDefaultPaymentSettingPO, mapper interface + XML, domain model + assembler under infrastructure/domain. Mirror UserInfoPO / UserInfoMapper.xml conventions.
Add experiment modulo + rollout config (default-payment-method.rollout-pct, kill-switch) in application.yml; compute treatment_group = gojekCustomerId % 2 when rollout active.

Implement internal REST: POST /user-service/internal/user/default-payment-method (upsert opt-in), GET .../default-payment-method, POST .../default-payment-method/apply (idempotent) in a new DefaultPaymentMethodInternalController. Add application + domain services.
Unit + integration tests for PUS endpoints (idempotency on apply, modulo distribution, kill-switch). WireMock Feign test for downstream absence.

Extend GplPaylaterUserServiceClient with getDefaultPaymentMethod + upsertDefaultPaymentMethod + applyDefaultPaymentMethod; add error decoder mappings (reuse GplPaylaterUserServiceClientConfiguration).
Enrich ToggleSettingService.fetchToggles to fetch default-payment-method status and merge into DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION; flip toggle-configs.json show behind a runtime FF.
Reroute ToggleSettingService.toggle for DEFAULT_PAYMENT_OPTION from bizend to PUS (optInSource=SETTINGS_PAGE, is_applied=true); leave other features on bizend.
Add BFF internal endpoint POST /internal/credit-application/default-payment-method/apply forwarding to PUS apply; wire into InternalController.
Update CreditApplicationServiceImpl submit path to forward optInDefault + treatment to PUS (US1 cache step).
Extend GopayLaterBffClient with applyDefaultPaymentMethod(...) (circuit-breaker protected) in lending-adapter.
Add 4th branch in PKYCCreditApplicationService.handleCreditApplicationCallback Mono.zip to call applyDefaultPaymentMethod only when status == SUCCESS. Keep existing 3 branches intact.
Add idempotency guard for the credit-application callback in lending-adapter (consult CallbackData.hasReceived before re-running side-effects) — reduces duplicate-apply risk.
Observability: New Relic/Sentry timings + custom metrics for PUS read/write/apply, BFF toggle enrichment, LA apply branch success/failure. Add dashboards + alerts for apply failure rate.
Contract tests across BFF↔PUS and LA↔BFF (WireMock / Reactor-test); add openspec entries in lending-adapter/openspec for the new apply call.
Feature-flag config + rollout plan execution (0% → 50% → 100%) and kill-switch verification in staging.
NFRs — PUS read <200ms p99; apply must not block post-credit flow beyond existing circuit-breaker budget
Apply runs in parallel Mono.zip with existing branches
Security — internal endpoints reachable only inside the cluster; BFF validates x-user-id from gateway
Data Privacy / PII — gofin_user_id is an internal identifier; no new PII column. No AES encryption needed (unlike user_info PII columns)
Compliance / Regulatory — default-payment-method is a UX preference, not a credit decision; sign-offs in PRD (Legal, Compliance, DPPO)
Risk & Fraud — setting default does not bypass risk checks; risk reject still blocks transactions (out-of-scope US9 confirms)
Setting is independent of risk evaluation
CS Escalation — new error codes from PUS apply path surfaced through BFF BffError enum; CS playbook update
Backward Compatibility — old clients not sending optInDefault default to false; toggle-configs.json stays show:false until FF on
US8 equivalent for the settings surface
Feature Flag / Kill Switch — default-payment-method.enabled (PUS + BFF) + rollout-pct
Kill switch returns show:false and skips apply branch
Observability — metrics for opt-in capture rate, apply success/failure, treatment split, read latency
Rate Limiting — reuse BFF ApiRateLimiter; PUS internal endpoints not exposed externally
Third-party / External — none; GoPay checkout propagation is separate (OQ-2)
Testing Strategy — unit (PUS modulo, idempotency), integration (BFF↔PUS, LA↔BFF WireMock), E2E (acquisition submit → approval → settings read)

ol.li: Phase 0 (staging): Deploy with default-payment-method.enabled=false. Verify DDL, endpoints, and contract tests. No user impact.
Phase 1 (prod, 0%): Enable flag for internal/test accounts only. Verify acquisition opt-in write, post-credit apply idempotency, and settings read.
Phase 2 (prod, 50%): Rollout-pct = 50 → treatment groups assigned; toggle-configs.json show:true for 50% of clients. Monitor apply failure rate, PUS read latency, and the experiment dashboard.
Phase 3 (prod, 100%): Rollout-pct = 100 after experiment concludes. Remove kill-switch only after sign-off.

p: Monitoring gate per phase: apply failure rate < 0.1%, PUS read p99 < 200ms, no increase in post-credit callback latency beyond existing budget.

ol.li: Immediate: set default-payment-method.enabled=false (kill-switch). BFF returns show:false; LA apply branch short-circuits; PUS endpoints return 200 but skip writes.
Data: the user_default_payment_setting table is additive; no destructive rollback needed. Rows can be ignored (read returns is_default_enabled=false when flag is off).
Client impact: toggles disappear; users who set default see no checkout change (GoPay propagation is independent).
Notify: GPL Engagement tech lead, PM (Andreas/Ignes), on-call.

p: TODO: capture the RFC review meeting outcome.

li.a: Mini PRD: Set GoPay Later as Default Payment Method across flows (Lark)

ul.li: Local PRD mirror: output/Default Payment Method/prd.md
Past PRD: Set GPL as default payment method in GPL onboarding
Repo: ~/project/paylater-user-service/ · ~/project/gpl-bff/ · ~/project/lending-adapter/

checkbox:
OQ-1 (US11 deviation): PRD US11 says "BFF fetches default method setting from GoPay real-time". Engineering context mandates the setting be owned by paylater-user-service (no GoPay API call).
Resolution: confirmed in this RFC — source of truth is paylater-user-service. PM to acknowledge the deviation. GoPay remains the owner of the actual checkout-widget "default" selection (see OQ-2).

OQ-2 (Checkout propagation): how does the GPL default flag in paylater-user-service reach the GoPay payment widget so GPL is pre-selected and labelled "Default" (US4)?
Resolution: out of scope for this RFC. Needs a separate integration with the GoPay payment-settings team — either GoPay polls/reads PUS, or PUS pushes to GoPay on apply.

OQ-3 (Post-approval screen write): when a user toggles on the post-approval screen (AC 3.2), is the write synchronous-blocking the screen, or fire-and-forget?
Resolution: TODO — recommend fire-and-forget with a retry, since the user may dismiss the screen.

OQ-4 (Experiment metric): minimum sample size and experiment duration for Treatment 1 vs 2 (US2).
Resolution: TODO — confirm with Product Analyst (Lisa/Azka).

OQ-5 (Idempotency in lending-adapter): the credit-application callback currently has no dedup guard. Should we add one as part of BE-13, or keep relying on PUS apply idempotency only?
Resolution: TODO — recommend both layers (defense in depth).

ul.li: TODO
